PERSONAL INJURY/PROPERTY DAMAGE - NON-VEHICLE RELATED, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PETERSON, CHRISTINE AS TO DEFENDANT DIAZ, PHILIP DIAZ, ARAYA CITY OF BERKELEY DOES 1 TO 10, INCLUSIVE SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR OCT-29-2010 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JUL-26-2010 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         OCT-14-2010

JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-30-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
PARTIES/ATTORNEYS FROM CONSOLIATED CASE NUMBER 500217 ADDED TO LEAD CASE NUMBER 494798 FOR MAILING OF COURT NOTICES.
MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-10-500217 WITH CASE CGC-09-494798, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION HEARING SET FOR AUG-19-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY OF BERKELEY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH CCP SECTION 394 CONFERS UPON A PUBLIC ENTITY THE RIGHT TO CHANGE OF VENUE TO THE COUNTY WHERE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED, THESE CASES MUST BE CONSOLIDATED TO AVOID PREJUDICE TO ALL PARTIES. THE CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HAS A COMPETING AND EQUAL RIGHT UNDER CCP SECTION 394 TO HAVE ITS CASE HEARD IN SAN FRANCISCO. THE ENDS OF JUSTICE DICTATE THAT THE CASES BE HEARD TOGETHER. (CCP SECTION 397). FURTHER, THE COURT GRANTED MR. OREBIC'S REQUEST FOR A STAY OF THE ACTION (INCLUDING DISCOVERY). PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: JOANNE FARRELL, CSR #4838
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-29-2010 CONTINUED TO DEC-30-2010 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212, FOR PLAINTIFF TO FILE ORDER GRANTING STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-16-2010 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-18-2010 Continued To Dec-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Plaintiff Christine Peterson'S Motion To Transfer
Off Calendar Sep-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Craig S. Dykman of Dykman & Wester (454.8545) for plaintiff Christine Peterson; Frederick P. Sheinfield of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3872) for the City and County of San Francisco; Matthew J. Orebic of the Office of the City Attorney (510.981.6998) for the city of Berkeley

Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CITY OF BERKELEY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO ALAMEDA COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT is denied. Although CCP section 394 confers upon a public entity the right to change of venue to the county where the accident occurred, these cases must be consolidated to avoid prejudice to all parties. The City & County of San Francisco has a competing and equal right under CCP section 394 to have its case heard in San Francisco. The ends of justice dictate that the cases be heard together. (CCP section 397). Further, the court granted Mr. Orebic's request for a stay of the action (including discovery). Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Joanne Farrell, CSR #4838

Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue To Alameda County Superior Court
Off Calendar Aug-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT CITY OF BERKELEY'S Motion To Transfer Venue To Alameda County Superior Court IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 3 0 2 AND TO BE HEARD CONCURRENTLY WITH THE MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE IN CASE C G C 09-494798 ON SEPTEMBER 17, 2010. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
